title: gnaphalium papillosum
intro: Gnaphalium papillosum, also known as woolly cudweed, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Gnaphalium papillosum include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestive health, and act as an antioxidant, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat minor skin irritations and digestive issues.
